mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer spending momentum, which drives corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% signals a zero-lower-bound liquidity trap that suppresses fixed-income yields and drives portfolio rebalancing into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in payrolls (PAYEMS) and decline in unemployment (UNRATE) signal a tightening labor market that boosts wage income and consumption, amplifying earnings growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns beyond either signal in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term of PAYEMS and UNRATE produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions because capacity constraints bind and output gains directly translate to margin expansion; this relationship is absent outside late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,200 thousand units signals contraction in residential investment and demand erosion, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1200, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of occurrences over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP level (GDPC1) quarterly surprises above trailing trend proxy aggregate demand shocks that reprice expected earnings upward and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 surprises above trend do not lead to positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and a low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal negative real rates that erode corporate margins and necessitate future tightening, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction of CPIAUCSL acceleration and low FEDFUNDS exhibits Rank IC greater than -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points over two months signals labor market deterioration that reduces disposable income and triggers risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp in 2 months coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered cases over a 3-year rolling window.
